reasoning: The 2025 Ivory Coast Presidential election is scheduled for late October 2025. Historically, highly contested elections in Ivory Coast (such as in 2010 and 2020) have resulted in sharp spikes in political violence, security deployments, and arrests—all of which fall under the ACLED 'Strategic developments' category. Since the 30-day average over the previous 360 days is a relatively modest 4.750, it indicates that the preceding year did not experience explosive, sustained tension that would set an impossibly high baseline. The focal point of the election will naturally generate a concentration of events (arrests, property destruction, security agreements/deployments) far exceeding the normal baseline during the exact 30-day periods corresponding to our first two resolution dates, which encompass the election day and the immediate aftermath (late October and November). Consequently, the likelihood of exceeding the 4.750 baseline is very high for 2025-11-02 and 2025-11-25. By late January 2026, the expected post-election stabilization raises uncertainty, bringing the expected event count back near or below the 4.75 baseline, increasing the uncertainty and shifting the probability closer to 0.5.
